title: x-tra rich gooey argentinian lamb empanadas
description: Showcase your culinary skills with these rich, gooey, and savory Argentinian lamb empanadas, featuring fragrant rocket and a hint of smokiness from tukbaegi-earthenware-cooked lamb.
instructions: Preheat the tukbaegi-earthenware vessel in a preheated oven at 220°C (425°F) for 10 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat 40g of butter over medium heat and cook the chopped onion until translucent, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 10 minutes.
Add the red wine, beef broth, tomato paste, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 hour, or until the lamb is tender.
Let the lamb cool slightly before shredding it with two forks.
In a separate bowl, combine the high-protein flour, beaten egg, and a pinch of salt. Mix until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 12cm (5in) in diameter.
Place a tablespoon of the shredded lamb and a sprinkle of chopped rocket onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and seal the edge with a fork. Brush the tops with a little water and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Cook the empanadas in the preheated tukbaegi-earthenware vessel for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown, and serve hot.
